name: Harrod's Christmas Pudding
recipeIngredient: 1/2 lb Unsalted butter plus about 2 tsp Butter, for greasing molds
1/3 cup Chopped mixed citrus peel (2 ounces)
recipeInstructions:
1. Thoroughly butter and dust with flour two 1-quart molds (or dome-shaped china, ceramic or glass bowls). Set aside.
2. Beat the 1/2 lb. butter until soft. Gradually add sugar and beat until fluffy.
3. Add eggs and syrup, continuing to beat until well combined.
4. Sift together the self-rising flour, salt, cinnamon, nutmeg, apple pie spice, lemon juice, grated rinds, bread crumbs, fruits (raisins, currants), and mixed citrus peel into the butter mixture. Mix gently until just incorporated.
5. Spoon the mixture into the prepared molds. If the molds don't have lids, cover each with a circle of waxed paper, then a piece of foil pleated across the center and securely tied in place.
6. Refrigerate overnight.
7. In the morning, place the molds in a large saucepan filled with enough water to come halfway up the sides of the molds. Cover and steam for 5 hours. Remove from the water.
8. Let cool completely. Cover with a clean piece of waxed paper and a pudding cloth (muslin or closely woven cheesecloth), securing tightly with string ties at both ends.
9. Leave in the refrigerator to mature until ready to serve.
10. Before serving, steam for an additional 3 hours. Remove from the mold and place on a serving plate.
11. Garnish with holly and ignite with brandy.
Yield: Two 2 lb. puddings.
